tickets.title: US001: Mobile food ordering
tickets.description:
As a student, I want to order food from my phone before arriving at the cafeteria so that I can reduce waiting time in line.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. The student can browse the menu on a mobile device.
2. The student can place an order before arriving at the cafeteria.
3. The order appears in the kitchen order queue after submission.

tickets.title: US002: Allergy-based menu filtering
tickets.description:
As a student with food allergies, I want to filter menu items based on allergens so that I only see foods that are safe for me to eat.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. The student can select one or more allergen filters.
2. Menu items containing the selected allergens are hidden from the menu.
3. The filtered menu updates immediately after a filter is applied.

tickets.title: US003: Sold-out menu management
tickets.description:
As a staff member, I want to mark menu items as sold out instantly so that students cannot order unavailable food items.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. The staff member can mark a menu item as sold out.
2. Sold-out items cannot be ordered by students.
3. The menu updates immediately to reflect the sold-out status.

tickets.title: US004: Sold out menu management
tickets.description:
As a staff member, I want to mark menu items as sold out instantly so that students cannot order unavailable food.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. The staff member can mark a menu item as sold out.
2. Sold out items are no longer available for new orders.
3. Students can see that the item is sold out on the menu.

tickets.title: US005: Daily special push notifications
tickets.description:
As a student, I want to receive push notifications about daily special discounts so that I can take advantage of cafeteria promotions.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. The system sends a push notification about the daily special promotion.
2. The notification includes the discount details.
3. The student can open the app from the notification to view the promoted menu item.

tickets.title: NFR Epic 1: Reliability: The kitchen display system must continue operating for at
tickets.description: Reliability: The kitchen display system must continue operating for at least 10 minutes during internet outages and synchronize when connectivity returns.

tickets.title: NFR Epic 2: Performance: Students should be able to place orders quickly to reduce
tickets.description: Performance: Students should be able to place orders quickly to reduce cafeteria queue times.

tickets.title: NFR Epic 3: Usability: Users should be able to easily filter menu items based on a
tickets.description: Usability: Users should be able to easily filter menu items based on allergies and dietary restrictions.

analysis.ambiguity1: The requirement to reduce the queue is too vague because it does not define a measurable target. The requirement does not specify how much the waiting time should be reduced or how success will be evaluated.
analysis.ambiguity2: The requirement to send push notifications offering discounts is vague because it does not specify which students should receive the notifications, how often they should be sent, or whether users can opt out.
analysis.conflictSolution: There is a conflict between the need for real-time order updates and the unreliable Wi-Fi in the cafeteria basement. The solution is to implement an offline mode that stores orders locally for up to 10 minutes and automatically synchronizes data when the connection is restored.
analysis.nfr1: Reliability: The kitchen display system must continue operating for at least 10 minutes during internet outages and synchronize when connectivity returns.
analysis.nfr2: Performance: Students should be able to place orders quickly to reduce cafeteria queue times.
analysis.nfr3: Usability: Users should be able to easily filter menu items based on allergies and dietary restrictions.
